mnemonics: Swing It Like JFrame: To build a Swing app, you start by extending JFrame (the main window). It's the 'frame' of your application picture.
keyPoints: Every Swing application must extend JFrame or JPanel to create a main window container.
The setDefaultCloseOperation(JFrame.EXIT_ON_CLOSE) method ensures the program terminates when the window is closed.
Components (like JButton, JLabel) are added to the content pane of the JFrame, not directly to the JFrame itself.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to call setVisible(true) on the JFrame, resulting in an invisible application.
Adding components directly to the JFrame instead of to its content pane (e.g., frame.add(component) instead of frame.getContentPane().add(component)).
Not placing the GUI creation and manipulation inside the Event Dispatch Thread (EDT) using SwingUtilities.invokeLater(), which can lead to thread-safety issues.
